svg.title: tmux workspace sidebar screenshot
svg.desc: Live tmux window showing the workspace sidebar alongside editor and Codex panes.
svg.text: If you want the fastest “next level” jump, I’d implement these three first: tree connectors, pane meta column, and a less noisy color system.
• I’m patching the live defaults now. The first pass will change hierarchy and information density, not just recolor the same layout.

└ context-mode.ctx_batch_execute({"commands":[{"label":"Sidebar App Tests","command":"cd \"/Users/ict/.config/tmux/plugins/tmux-workspace-sidebar\" && sed -n '1,360p' tests/test_sidebar_app.py"},{"label":"Sidebar Tree Tests","command":"cd \"/Users/ict/.config/tmux/plugins/tmux-workspace-sidebar\" && sed -n

'1,320p' tests/test_sidebar_tree.py"}],"queries":["Which tests cover header text, row rendering, meta columns, and indicators?","Which tests will need updates if tree prefixes or pane meta are changed?","Are there existing tests around row.render, session/window/pane labels, or
Executed 2 commands (633 lines, 22.7KB). Indexed 7 sections. Searched 3 queries.
## Which tests cover header text, row rendering, meta columns, and indicators?
• I’m editing the tree model and renderer together so the new look is structural: connectors, cleaner labels, pane meta, tighter spacing, and a stronger header bar.
